content.full_text:
Letter from Dr. Charlie M. Knight to the Secretary of State, Political Reform Division

April 24, 1992

Secretary of State
Political Reform Division
P.O. Box 1467
Sacramento, California 95807

Dear Sir or Madam:

This letter is submitted to request assistance with a unique situation regarding campaign reporting. I served on the Compton Community College Board, Area 3, and ran for reelection in November 1991. I filed a Form 410 because my expenses were under $1000. However, I participated in a re-count in December 1991 and the amount required to pay for the re-count was $500. Because of the $500 cost of the re-count, this caused my campaign expenses to exceed $1000. Before participating in the re-count I asked if this would effect my campaign expenses and was told that it wouldn’t because it was after the campaign. However, after attending a recent workshop I was informed that the cost of a re-count was considered a campaign expense. I am, therefore, contacting you for advice or suggestions of how to re-file my campaign expenses. Please send me the necessary instructions and form to accomplish this task. Thank you for any consideration that you may give this request.

Sincerely,

[Signature]
Dr. Charlie M. Knight

CMK:dym
